steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The blank describes the relationship between seismic monitoring and orbital imaging. The colon introduces an explanation: seismic monitoring narrows the search area; orbital imaging then pinpoints the exact crater within that area. Each method does what the other cannot.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
A. exhaustive — exhaustive means covering every possibility completely. Plug in: "their roles are exhaustive" — this would mean together they cover everything with no gaps, but the passage is about how they work together sequentially, not about completeness. Eliminated.

B. redundant — redundant means unnecessarily repetitive, doing the same job twice. Plug in: "their roles are redundant" — but they do different things (seismic locates roughly; orbital locates precisely). They are not doing the same job. Eliminated.

C. interchangeable — interchangeable means either one could substitute for the other. Plug in: "their roles are interchangeable" — but you can't use seismic monitoring to pinpoint a crater precisely, nor orbital imaging to detect vibrations. They cannot swap roles. Eliminated.

D. complementary — complementary means each supplies what the other lacks, together forming a complete whole. Plug in: "their roles are complementary" — seismic gives rough location, orbital gives precision; neither works alone. Correct.
